key_insight: Unified Gradient Projection (UGP) balances multilingual gradients during continual fine-tuning, reducing dominant-language bias and catastrophic forgetting in low-resource ASR.
engineering_relevance: For practical multilingual ASR deployment, UGP offers a training strategy that preserves prior language performance while adapting to new low-resource languages more stably.
method: Build a unified projection space from reference gradients sampled via language-balanced replay.
Project parameter updates to limit cross-task interference and equalize per-language contributions.
Combine gradient-level projection with data replay for complementary stability and plasticity gains.
evidence_signal: Across multiple low-resource language groups and model scales, the method improves adaptation and greatly reduces forgetting, with near-zero average forgetting reported on Whisper-large-v3.
watch_item: The approach depends on replay quality and balanced language sampling, so performance may be sensitive to the replay buffer design and compute overhead.
paper_title: Unified Gradient Projection: Language-Balanced Continual Learning for Multilingual Low-Resource ASR
